skill.name: journal-skills
skill.description: Recommends target journals for manuscript submission by analyzing the paper topic/abstract and the journal distribution of similar PubMed literature; use when users ask for journal recommendation/matching, submission strategy, PubMed search, or similar-literature statistics.
skill.summary: PubMed-powered journal recommender for manuscript submission planning
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 34 static findings from 5 scanned files (674 lines). All CRITICAL, HIGH, and MEDIUM severity findings are false positives attributable to the skill's legitimate function as a PubMed journal recommendation tool. The 'dangerous combination' heuristic (code execution + network + credentials) identifies expected operational patterns for NCBI E-utilities access, not malicious behavior. The 'external_commands' findings are markdown documentation with example bash commands, not active code execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding at SKILL.md:3 is a misidentification of YAML frontmatter text. The 'system reconnaissance' findings reference standard academic practices for avoiding predatory journals. LOW env_access findings are confirmed but reflect documented, expected NCBI API key usage with placeholder values and no exfiltration risk. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, obfuscation, or prompt injection found.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for NCBI API credentials
low_findings.description: The script accesses CONFIG["API_KEY"] and CONFIG["EMAIL"] for authenticating with NCBI E-utilities. This is expected and documented behavior for PubMed API access. Values are placeholders in the default config. No credential exfiltration risk - API calls go exclusively to legitimate NCBI endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network calls to NCBI E-utilities API
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Uses Python urllib to call public NCBI PubMed API endpoints (esearch, esummary). This is the core legitimate function of the skill. Endpoints are hardcoded to official NCBI domains. No data exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command examples in markdown documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Backtick-wrapped commands in SKILL.md are documentation code blocks showing example usage (e.g., 'python scripts/pubmed_journal_recommender.py'). These are not executable code but markdown formatting for human readers.

dangerous_patterns.title: System reconnaissance false detection
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE POSITIVE: References in audit_result_v1.json and guide.md to 'predatory journals' and quality checks are standard academic publishing advice. Not reconnaissance. The audit_result_v1.json file is an earlier scanner output, not malicious content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm misidentification
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 for 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. Line 3 is YAML frontmatter containing 'description: Recommends target journals...'. No cryptographic operation exists in this file.

content.user_title: Find the Best Journal for Your Research Paper
content.value_statement: Researchers often spend weeks finding suitable journals for their manuscripts. This skill analyzes your paper abstract against PubMed literature to recommend matching target journals with evidence-based statistics.
actual_capabilities: Extracts key research keywords from manuscript abstracts
Searches PubMed for similar published literature
Computes journal frequency distributions from search results
Generates ranked journal recommendations with match counts
Outputs results in structured JSON and CSV formats
limitations: Only searches PubMed; does not include Scopus, Web of Science, or other databases
Requires NCBI API access with a valid email address
Limited to the top 50 PubMed results per search query
Does not verify journal scope, editorial policies, or review timelines

use_cases.title: First Manuscript Submission Guidance
use_cases.description: An early-career researcher has completed their first paper and needs help identifying suitable journals. This skill analyzes the abstract against PubMed literature to produce a ranked shortlist of journals where similar work has been published.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students and postdoctoral researchers submitting their first manuscript

use_cases.title: Exploring New Publication Venues
use_cases.description: An established author wants to expand their publication options beyond familiar journals. The skill searches PubMed for papers similar to their new research and reveals journals they may not have considered.
use_cases.target_user: Experienced researchers looking for new journal options

use_cases.title: Team Publication Strategy Planning
use_cases.description: A research group needs to plan submission strategy for multiple manuscripts. The skill generates data-driven journal recommendations that help the team decide primary targets, backup options, and submission sequencing.
use_cases.target_user: Research group leaders and lab managers coordinating publication plans

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Recommendation by Title
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to find suitable journals for my manuscript titled '[insert paper title here]'. Can you analyze this title and recommend journals where similar research has been published?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has only a paper title and wants initial journal suggestions

prompt_templates.title: Abstract-Based Journal Matching
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is the abstract of my manuscript: [paste abstract]. Please extract the key research topics, search PubMed for similar publications, and identify the most common journals publishing in this area. Rank them by relevance and provide match counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a completed abstract and wants evidence-based journal matching

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Journal Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: My manuscript is about [topic]. I am considering submitting to [Journal A], [Journal B], or [Journal C]. Search PubMed for papers similar to my topic and tell me how many matching papers each journal has published. Include the scope fit assessment for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a shortlist of candidate journals and needs data to compare them

prompt_templates.title: Full Publication Strategy with Alternatives
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a complete submission strategy for my paper about [topic]. Run the PubMed journal analysis, give me a primary target and two backup journals with matching rationale. For each journal mention scope fit, audience alignment, and any risks I should consider before submitting.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs a structured submission plan with risk assessment

output_examples.input: I have a paper about machine learning for medical image diagnosis. Can you recommend journals?
output: 2. IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging - 9 matching papers
3. Journal of Biomedical Informatics - 7 matching papers
4. Computers in Biology and Medicine - 5 matching papers
A CSV file with the full ranked list and a JSON file with detailed query information have been saved to the output directory.

output_examples.input: Compare Journal A and Journal B for my paper about CRISPR gene editing in cardiovascular disease.
output: PubMed search found 35 similar papers related to CRISPR gene editing in cardiovascular research.
Journal A: 8 matching papers - strong scope alignment with cardiovascular genetics.
Journal B: 3 matching papers - relevant but publishes fewer gene-editing studies.
Recommendation: Journal A as primary target given higher concentration of related work. Journal B as backup option.
Note: Verify open access policies and review timelines before submitting.

best_practices: Provide a detailed abstract for more accurate keyword extraction and better matching results
Use an NCBI API key to increase query rate limits and avoid throttling during PubMed searches
Always verify journal scope, editorial policies, and review timelines before submitting your manuscript
anti_patterns: Relying only on journal impact factor without checking scope alignment with your research topic
Submitting to journals without verifying they are not predatory or non-compliant with ethical standards
Using vague or incomplete abstracts that produce irrelevant keyword matches and poor recommendations

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for a journal recommendation?
faq.answer: At minimum provide your paper title. For better results include the full abstract so the skill can extract more accurate keywords for the PubMed search.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee my paper will be accepted by the recommended journals?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides data-driven suggestions based on where similar papers were published. Acceptance depends on journal scope, paper quality, and editorial decisions.

faq.question: Why do I need an NCBI API key?
faq.answer: NCBI E-utilities require an email address for access. An API key is optional but recommended for higher request rate limits and faster searches.

faq.question: Can this skill search databases other than PubMed?
faq.answer: No. This skill only searches PubMed. It does not access Scopus, Web of Science, Google Scholar, or other academic databases.

faq.question: How many PubMed results does the skill search?
faq.answer: The skill retrieves up to 50 PubMed records per query and analyzes their journal distribution to produce recommendations.

faq.question: Does the skill check if a journal is predatory?
faq.answer: No. The skill only reports statistical matches from PubMed. You should independently verify that any recommended journal is legitimate and not predatory.
